criteria.question: Critique is explicitly tied to a concrete document section or checklist item and is not an abstract statement.
Critique is grounded in a retrieved source or a concrete fragment of the current document rather than generic industry practice.
Critic does not provide a ready-made solution and does not violate the direct-answer constraint.
Critic's claim is factually correct and matches expert consensus rather than being false critique.
Critic correctly identifies cross-section contradictions when they are present and does not invent extra ones.
The wording is clear, constructive, and explains the consequence of the problem or the area to reconsider.
When context is insufficient, Critic states that explicitly instead of inventing facts or criticizing nonexistent content.
The critique does not duplicate other critical comments in the same session.
The critique avoids excessive detail that could confuse the user and stays focused on the main issue.
